title: Hello World Server
subtitle: Build a real web server in 10 lines of code

sections.content:
Time to build something real — a web server in 10 lines of code.

Every website you visit works because a **server** is listening for requests and sending back responses. When you type a URL in your browser, your browser sends a request to a server, and the server sends back the web page.

You're about to build one of those servers. It will run on your own computer, and you'll be able to visit it in your browser. This is the same technology that powers Netflix, Twitter, and millions of other web apps.
keyPoints: A server listens for requests and sends responses
Your server will run locally at http://localhost:3000

sections.instruction: Create a file called `app.js` with the following code. You can copy the entire block — this is a complete Express web server. Make sure you're in your `my-server` folder where you installed Express.

troubleshooting.solution: Make sure you ran npm install express in the same folder. Check with: ls node_modules/express

troubleshooting.problem: I don't know how to create the file
troubleshooting.solution: Open any text editor, paste the code, and save it as app.js in your my-server folder. Or use: nano app.js in the terminal, paste the code, then press Ctrl+O to save and Ctrl+X to exit.

sections.instruction: Start your server! Run the file with Node. You should see a message confirming the server is running.

troubleshooting.solution: Port 3000 is already in use by another program. Either stop that program or change the port number in app.js to 3001.
Make sure you're in the my-server folder and that you ran npm install express earlier. Check with: ls node_modules/express

sections.instruction:
Open your web browser and go to **http://localhost:3000**. You should see your message displayed on the page!

When you're done admiring your work, go back to your terminal and press **Ctrl+C** to stop the server.

troubleshooting.solution: Make sure your server is still running in the terminal (you should see the 'Server running' message). Don't close that terminal window.

troubleshooting.problem: I can't type in the terminal anymore
troubleshooting.solution: That's because the server is running! It takes over the terminal while it's active. Press Ctrl+C to stop it and get your prompt back.

completionMessage: You just built a web server! A real, working server running on your machine. You installed Node.js, learned npm, created a project, installed a package, and built something that responds to web browsers. That's a huge milestone. You're not just learning about programming anymore — you're doing it.
